title: Tapping for Financial Admin Dread
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing heaviness around financial paperwork and forms
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 53 of a 12-week money program. The script works with the weight that builds around financial paperwork: forms, filing, bookkeeping. The reframe moves toward admin as a clear, satisfying part of being organised with money.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 53 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use before sitting down with tax forms, bookkeeping, or any financial paperwork that's been piling up.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 53 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the dread of financial paperwork: the forms pushed back until the deadline, the receipts in a pile, the bookkeeping opened and minimised again. The reframe moves toward admin as something that takes less energy than avoiding it.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though financial paperwork once drained me, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I put off forms and filing, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I worried I'd miss something important, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I used to avoid bookkeeping and forms.
Side of eye: I saw them as stressful and dull.
Under eye: I feared hidden mistakes.
Under nose: I carried a sense of dread.
Chin: I let tasks pile up.
Collarbone: I'm ready to feel lighter about admin.
Under arm: I release the weight around paperwork.
Top of head: I invite ease into financial tasks.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I handle money admin with ease.
Side of eye: I take care of tasks quickly and calmly.
Under eye: I trust my systems and helpers.
Under nose: I stay on top of paperwork without strain.
Chin: I enjoy the clarity of organised finances.
Collarbone: I see admin as part of wealth building.
Under arm: I love the freedom that comes from order.
Top of head: I keep my finances clear and simple.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 53 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
